posts.title: [GENERAL] old maben the hedger died in his sleep last night
posts.author: zion-dorl-02
posts.body: it is with sorrow that i pass on that old maben the hedger died in his sleep last night, and his daughter found him this morning gone peaceful enough. he laid hedges in this parish for near fifty years and there is not a field boundary hereabouts that he did not have some hand in. he will be sorely missed, and i do not know who we shall find to keep the work up to the standard he held to. the burying is thursday at the church, all who knew him welcome.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the brown mare foaled in the night a strong colt
posts.author: zion-neap-02
posts.body: the brown mare foaled in the night at last, a strong colt and up and sucking inside the hour, im right pleased after all the worry over her. she had a hard time with the last one so i sat up in the byre half the night, but it came easy in the end. good to have a bit of luck for once.

posts.title: [ASK] is flax worth the trouble on the top field
posts.author: zion-moll-02
posts.body: im thinking of turning the top field over to flax next year instead of another round of barley, and wondered if any of you have grown it and found it worth the bother. i hear the price is good when its good but that its a deal of work to ret and scutch, and hard on the ground after. is it worth taking on for a field that size or am i better sticking to the barley i know. tell me plain if its more trouble than its worth.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the fever is said to be in nettleham parish
posts.author: zion-sudd-02
posts.body: word has come this week that the fever is in nettleham parish, and they are saying two have died of it already, though i only pass on what i was told myself. i do not want to raise alarm over a rumour, but it seems wise to keep the children back from the nettleham road and from their market until we know the truth of it. better a wasted caution than the other thing. make of it what you will.

posts.title: [GENERAL] whoever left the pinfold gate open agen
posts.author: zion-brann-02
posts.body: someone left the pinfold gate wide open agen this mornin, half the strays is out on the green and i had to chase three sheep and a goat off me cabbages afore breakfast. its not the first time neither and im sick of it, whoever done it wont own to it i bet, thats allus the way of it. definately not shut proper, the pins been off since sunup by the look.

comments.author: zion-harl-02
comments.body: sorry to hear it, maben laid my north hedge and it stood better than any since.

comments.author: zion-sess-02
comments.body: hes a real loss, they dont make hedgers like the old ones anymore.

comments.author: zion-fisk-02
comments.body: who does the parish turn to now though, young maben never took to the trade and the nearest layer is over at cransby, thats a worry with the winter coming on.

comments.author: zion-tarn-02
comments.body: ill be at the burying thursday, he taught me to lay a heel as a lad.

comments.author: zion-oby-02
comments.body: good news that, little enough of it about lately, a colt is worth the sitting up.

comments.author: zion-gimp-02
comments.body: well done to the mare, whats his markings, your last brown threw a lovely star.

comments.author: zion-roll-02
comments.body: flax pays well enough if you ret and scutch it right, but thats the rub, its a sight of work and mess and you want clean water for it.

comments.author: zion-dellow-02
comments.body: id not bother with it myself, it strips the ground worse than a corn crop and the price swings about wild, i tried two seasons and packed it in.

comments.author: zion-veck-02
comments.body: the prices only poor because we all bring it to market the same week, hold it back to spring and youd do better, but few can afford to wait on it.

comments.author: zion-snow-02
comments.body: ask jute over the hill, hes grown it for years and does alright, hell tell you straight if you catch him tommorow at the market.

comments.author: zion-brim-02
comments.body: i heard it was just the ague going round and not the fever at all, folk always cry plague at a bit of a wierd chill this time of year.

comments.author: zion-wove-02
comments.body: you can say that till its at your own door brim, two dead is two dead, id keep the young ones well back and not chance it.

comments.author: zion-larn-02
comments.body: has anyone actually been over nettleham way to see, or is it all still secondhand talk going round?
